Bug / Task Description: ORSet and ORMap (pkg/crdt) do not converge correctly when removals are involved. An element removed on one replica reappears after that replica merges state from another replica that still holds it, and ORMap can resurrect deleted keys the same way, so removals are effectively lost across the gossip/merge path. Fix convergence so that a removal observed on one replica is never undone by subsequent merges, across any number of replicas and regardless of the order in which replicas exchange state. Two invariants must be preserved: add-wins semantics (a concurrent add carrying state the remover never observed must survive a remove), and correct last-writer-wins value resolution for ORMap entries. Do not change exported names, signatures, or serialization formats, and confine changes to pkg/crdt.
Commit Subject: fix(crdt): correct observed-remove convergence in ORSet and ORMap
